# Financial Deep Research: QXO Inc (QXO)

Research conducted: 2026-05-05  
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 will note the absence

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
The most recent reported period is the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, released on February 25, 2026. As of the research date (May 5, 2026), QXO has not yet reported first-quarter 2026 results; the earnings announcement is expected in mid-May 2026. [Source: Public.com / Title: QXO Inc (QXO) Earnings / Date: 2026-05-04]*

For the three months ended December 31, 2025, QXO reported net sales of $2,194.1 million, gross profit of $529.9 million (24.2% gross margin), a GAAP net loss of $(90.2) million, adjusted EBITDA of $150.3 million (6.9% margin), and adjusted diluted EPS of $0.02. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

### Most Recent Full Year Report
For the full year 2025, QXO generated net sales of $6,842.2 million, gross profit of $1,572.7 million (23.0% gross margin), and adjusted gross profit of $1,704.4 million (24.9% adjusted gross margin). The company reported a GAAP net loss of $(279.4) million and adjusted EBITDA of $647.8 million (9.5% margin). Adjusted diluted EPS was $0.34, while the GAAP basic and diluted loss per common share was $(0.63). [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

These results include the legacy Beacon Roofing Supply, Inc. operations from the date of acquisition on April 29, 2025 through December 31, 2025—approximately eight months of contribution. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
Revenue in 2025 was driven almost entirely by the Beacon acquisition. Organic growth is difficult to isolate because QXO was a pre-revenue platform prior to the Beacon deal and has been integrating the business since late April 2025. Management noted that fourth-quarter results were in line with pre-announcements and that operational execution is focused on integration, technology investment, and sales capacity additions. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

Comparisons to Beacon’s standalone prior-year performance show some near-term compression: Beacon’s fourth quarter of 2024 standalone net sales were approximately $2.4 billion with adjusted EBITDA of $222.5 million, implying a higher quarterly EBITDA margin than QXO’s reported Q4 2025 result. [Source: MDM / Title: QXO issues $750M stock offering, preliminary 4Q financials / Date: 2026-01-16]* This suggests integration costs, softer residential demand, or deliberate growth investments are weighing on near-term profitability.

### Segment Performance
QXO operates as a single reportable segment: the wholesale distribution of building materials. For the year ended December 31, 2025, revenue composition was as follows:
- Residential roofing products: $3,307.1 million (48.3% of net sales)
- Non-residential roofing products: $1,883.9 million (27.5%)
- Complementary building products: $1,592.7 million (23.3%)
- Software products and services: $58.5 million (0.9%) [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

The company does not disclose separate profitability by product line, so segment margin contribution is unavailable.

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Operations are principally within the United States, and the company has only minimal foreign currency exposure. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]*** As of December 31, 2025, foreign currency translation adjustments were negligible ($2.3 million other comprehensive loss). [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
A two-year historical lookback is largely meaningless for QXO because the company was a shell/platform with only $56.9 million in sales in 2024. The 2025 revenue base of $6.8 billion is entirely acquisition-driven. Therefore, the trajectory must be assessed on a pro forma, run-rate basis rather than reported historical comparables.

On a pro forma basis, the growth path is explosive but inorganic:
- **Beacon Roofing Supply** (acquired April 29, 2025): Beacon generated $9,763.2 million in net sales in 2024. [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply / Title: FY2024 Earnings Presentation / Date: 2025-02-27]*** On an annualized basis, this is a ~$9.8 billion revenue platform.
- **Kodiak Building Partners** (acquired April 1, 2026): Kodiak generated roughly $2.4 billion in revenue and approximately $211 million in 2025 EBITDA. [Source: HousingWire / Title: QXO closes on $2.25 billion Kodiak Building Partners deal / Date: 2026-04-01]* This adds a second major platform in lumber, trusses, and structural products.
- **TopBuild Corp.** (definitive agreement announced April 19, 2026; expected close Q3 2026): TopBuild generated approximately $6.2 billion in net sales and $1.14 billion in adjusted EBITDA in 2025. [Source: Quartz / Title: QXO acquiring TopBuild in $17 billion building products deal / Date: 2026-04-20]*

If all transactions close as planned, QXO’s run-rate revenue would approach $18 billion, consistent with management’s statement that the combined company would have “more than $18 billion of combined company revenue and more than $2 billion of combined company adjusted EBITDA.” [Source: Quartz / Title: QXO acquiring TopBuild in $17 billion building products deal / Date: 2026-04-20]*

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
Beacon’s standalone 2024 revenue growth was 7.1%, with organic volume up an estimated 1–2% and price up 1–2%. [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply / Title: FY2024 Earnings Presentation / Date: 2025-02-27]*** TopBuild has grown sales at a 10-year CAGR of approximately 13%. [Source: Market Chameleon / Title: QXO’s $17 Billion TopBuild Acquisition / Date: 2026-04-20]* By comparison, QXO’s headline revenue growth is not organic; it is a roll-up. The key question for the trajectory is whether the company can sustain low-single-digit organic growth in the legacy businesses while layering on accretive acquisitions.

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

Because QXO is a distributor, the most meaningful profitability metrics are gross margin, SG&A leverage, and adjusted EBITDA margin. Net income is currently distorted by acquisition-related amortization, stock-based compensation, and transaction costs.

For the full year 2025, QXO reported a gross margin of 23.0% and an adjusted gross margin of 24.9%. The difference is driven by $131.7 million in inventory fair value adjustments related to purchase accounting. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2025-11-06]*** Adjusted EBITDA margin was 9.5%, which is in line with Beacon’s standalone 2024 adjusted EBITDA margin of 9.5%. [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply 10-K / Title: FY2024 Annual Report / Date: 2025-02-27]***

However, quarterly margins have been volatile. Q4 2025 adjusted EBITDA margin was only 6.9%, down from 11.1% in Q3 2025. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]*** This appears to reflect seasonality, integration investments, and mix shifts.

SG&A expense (excluding stock-based compensation) was $1,250.3 million in 2025, or 18.3% of sales. This is higher than Beacon’s standalone 2024 SG&A ratio of 16.8% of sales, indicating duplicative overhead, acquired-branch costs, and technology investments that have not yet been synergized. [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply 10-K / Title: FY2024 Annual Report / Date: 2025-02-27]*** Stock-based compensation added another $144.5 million (2.1% of sales). [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

### Peer-Relative Profitability Context
TopBuild’s 2025 adjusted EBITDA margin is approximately 18.4% ($1.14 billion on $6.2 billion in sales). [Source: Quartz / Title: QXO acquiring TopBuild in $17 billion building products deal / Date: 2026-04-20]* This is nearly double legacy Beacon’s margin. If QXO can apply its procurement and technology playbook to TopBuild, there is meaningful margin-upside potential; conversely, if QXO’s integration overhead drags TopBuild’s margins down, the deal economics would weaken.

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
QXO generated $261.4 million in net cash from operating activities in 2025, compared with a GAAP net loss of $(279.4) million. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]*** The reconciliation is dominated by non-cash add-backs: depreciation ($108.4 million), amortization ($314.7 million), stock-based compensation ($144.5 million), and non-cash lease expense ($88.0 million). [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

Capital expenditures were $78.2 million (1.1% of sales), yielding free cash flow of roughly $183 million before M&A. This is a low-capex, distribution model. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

However, traditional free cash flow is not the cleanest lens for QXO because the company is an acquisition platform. The cash flow statement is dominated by investing outflows: $10.6 billion for acquisitions in 2025. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]*** Investors should focus on post-acquisition, pre-M&A operating cash flow conversion and the sustainability of EBITDA-to-cash conversion.

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital is material to this distributor model. As of December 31, 2025:
- Accounts receivable, net: $1,145.1 million
- Inventories, net: $1,497.3 million
- Vendor rebates receivable: $427.0 million
- Accounts payable and accrued expenses: $1,393.3 million ($819.0M + $574.3M) [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

In 2025, operating cash flow benefited from inventory reduction ($287.8 million) and accounts receivable collection ($159.8 million), but was partially offset by a vendor rebates receivable build ($186.8 million) and a paydown of accounts payable and accrued expenses ($326.4 million). [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]*** The vendor rebate receivable and payable dynamics suggest working capital management is a focus area, and cash conversion could fluctuate with supplier terms and inventory positioning.

### Earnings Quality
GAAP earnings are currently of low quality for valuation purposes because they are loaded with acquisition accounting noise. Full-year 2025 amortization was $314.7 million, stock-based compensation was $144.5 million, and transaction/transformation costs were significant. Goodwill and acquired intangibles totaled $8.9 billion ($5.1 billion goodwill + $3.8 billion intangibles), meaning amortization and potential impairment risk will remain elevated. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]*** Adjusted EBITDA and adjusted EPS are the metrics management and the Street appear to be using, but these exclude real costs (SBC, transaction expenses) that should be considered in a bull/bear case.

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

As of December 31, 2025, QXO carried:
- Cash and cash equivalents: $2,361.6 million
- Total current assets: $5,546.3 million
- Total assets: $15,887.3 million
- Total current liabilities: $1,550.0 million
- Total stockholders’ equity: $9,706.8 million [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
Outstanding debt as of year-end 2025 was $3,100.0 million gross ($3,057.3 million net):
- **Senior Secured Notes**: $2,250.0 million aggregate principal, 6.75% coupon, due April 30, 2032. Interest paid semi-annually. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***
- **Term Loan Facility**: $850.0 million original principal, variable rate based on Term SOFR plus margin (weighted-average effective rate of 5.72% as of December 31, 2025), due April 30, 2032. Requires quarterly amortization of 1.0% annually. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***
- **ABL Facility**: Approximately $1.97 billion available for additional borrowing as of December 31, 2025, with no outstanding borrowings (excluding $21.2 million in letters of credit). [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

There are no near-term maturities; the first material maturity is 2032. This is a favorable structure for an acquisition roll-up.

### Assessment
On a reported basis, QXO is in a net cash position ($2.36 billion cash vs. $3.06 billion net debt implies modest net leverage). However, this picture changes materially with the pending TopBuild acquisition and the already-closed Kodiak acquisition.

- **Kodiak** (closed April 1, 2026) was funded with $2.0 billion in cash plus 13.2 million QXO shares. [Source: HousingWire / Title: QXO closes on $2.25 billion Kodiak Building Partners deal / Date: 2026-04-01]* This likely consumed a meaningful portion of year-end cash and/or the January 2026 equity raises.
- **TopBuild** (pending) is a $17.0 billion transaction, structured as approximately 45% cash ($7.65 billion) and 55% stock. QXO has secured $6.0 billion in committed financing, including a $3.0 billion senior secured term loan and a $3.0 billion bridge facility. [Source: TradingView / Title: QXO to Acquire TopBuild in Cash-and-Stock Deal; Secures $6B Debt Commitments / Date: 2026-04-20]** The cash component is expected to be funded with the committed debt, cash on hand, and the $3.0 billion Series C convertible preferred equity commitment from Apollo and Temasek. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion / Date: 2026-01-12]***

Pro forma for TopBuild and Kodiak, total debt could approach $9 billion or more. Against a combined run-rate EBITDA of roughly $2.3 billion (Beacon ~$930M + Kodiak ~$211M + TopBuild ~$1,140M), leverage would likely be in the 3.5x–4.5x range. This is manageable for a distributor but represents a significant increase from the current net-cash position and would reduce financial flexibility if housing markets deteriorate.

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
No share repurchases were executed in 2025. The company has been an issuer of equity, not a buyer.

### Dividends
QXO does not pay a common stock dividend. The cash flow statement shows no common dividend in 2025. The company does pay dividends on preferred securities:
- Convertible Preferred Stock dividends: $90.0 million in 2025
- Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock dividends: $14.8 million in 2025 [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

### M&A / Capex
Capital deployment is overwhelmingly oriented toward M&A:
- **FY2025**: $10.6 billion for acquisitions (Beacon), $78.2 million in capex. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***
- **January 2026**: $750 million common stock offering at $23.80 per share. [Source: MDM / Title: QXO issues $750M stock offering, preliminary 4Q financials / Date: 2026-01-16]***
- **January 2026**: Upsized $3.0 billion Series C convertible preferred equity commitment from Apollo, Temasek, and others, contingent on acquisitions. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion / Date: 2026-01-12]***
- **April 2026**: Kodiak closed for $2.25 billion. [Source: HousingWire / Title: QXO closes on $2.25 billion Kodiak Building Partners deal / Date: 2026-04-01]***
- **Pending**: TopBuild for ~$17 billion, expected Q3 2026. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion / Date: 2026-04-19]***

### Returns on Capital
Current GAAP returns on capital are negative due to the net loss and massive goodwill base. Meaningful incremental returns will only become visible as integration synergies are realized. Management has guided to $300 million in run-rate synergies from the TopBuild deal by 2030. [Source: Quartz / Title: QXO acquiring TopBuild in $17 billion building products deal / Date: 2026-04-20]* For Kodiak, management claims an implied purchase multiple of 7.3x EBITDA including synergies, versus 10.7x on a standalone basis. [Source: HousingWire / Title: QXO closes on $2.25 billion Kodiak Building Partners deal / Date: 2026-04-01]* Whether these returns exceed QXO’s cost of capital depends on execution and the ultimate leverage profile.

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance
QXO did not issue explicit quantitative financial guidance for full-year 2026 in its February 2026 earnings release. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]***

### Most Recent Report Guidance
No quarterly guidance was provided for Q1 2026. The company expects to report Q1 2026 results in mid-May 2026. [Source: Public.com / Title: QXO Inc (QXO) Earnings / Date: 2026-05-04]*

### Management Commentary
On the Q4 2025 call and in subsequent press releases, Chairman and CEO Brad Jacobs highlighted:
- Integration of Beacon is “executing against our integration plan,” supported by technology, sales capacity, and long-term initiatives.
- With Kodiak, QXO grew its EBITDA run rate to more than $1 billion in under 10 months.
- The acquisition pipeline “remains very active,” and the company is “firmly on track to achieve $50 billion in annual revenue” within the next decade.
- Kodiak is expected to be “highly accretive to QXO’s 2026 earnings.”
- TopBuild will make QXO the second-largest publicly traded building products distributor in North America. [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results / Date: 2026-02-25]*** [Source: QXO, Inc. / Title: QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion / Date: 2026-04-19]***

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting
- **Kodiak closure timing and accretion (2026)**: **High** confidence on timing (already closed April 1, 2026); **Medium** confidence on accretion magnitude because integration has just begun.
- **TopBuild closure timing (Q3 2026)**: **Medium** confidence. The deal requires shareholder and regulatory approvals, though boards have unanimously approved and financing is committed.
- **TopBuild synergy realization ($300M by 2030)**: **Low-Medium** confidence. Synergy targets in large cross-industry distributions are often back-loaded and execution-dependent.
- **$50B revenue target within a decade**: **Low** confidence as a precise forecast. It implies another ~$30B+ of acquisitions beyond TopBuild and depends on continued access to capital markets.

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

- **Serial Integration Risk**: QXO is integrating Beacon while digesting Kodiak and preparing for TopBuild within a 12-month window. Operational disruption, customer attrition, or systems failures could impair margins.
- **Goodwill & Intangible Asset Risk**: The balance sheet carries $5.1 billion of goodwill and $3.8 billion of intangibles (mainly customer relationships and trade names). [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]*** A downturn in housing or commercial construction could trigger impairment charges.
- **Interest Rate & Refinancing Risk**: The Term Loan is floating-rate (5.72% as of year-end). The $6 billion TopBuild financing package includes a $3 billion bridge facility that will need to be refinanced with permanent capital before closing. Rising rates could increase pro forma interest expense. [Source: TradingView / Title: QXO to Acquire TopBuild in Cash-and-Stock Deal; Secures $6B Debt Commitments / Date: 2026-04-20]**
- **Cyclical Exposure**: Residential roofing is tied to storms, housing starts, and repair-and-remodel activity. Beacon’s own 2025 outlook noted softer residential demand and sluggish single-family starts. [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply / Title: FY2024 Earnings Presentation / Date: 2025-02-27]***
- **Dilution**: The TopBuild deal is 55% stock, and the Series C preferred is convertible. Existing common shareholders face significant dilution.
- **Vendor Rebate Receivables**: The $427 million vendor rebates receivable is a non-standard working capital item; changes in supplier programs could affect cash flow. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***

## 9. Discrepancies vs. Structured Data Baseline

Stage 1 baseline not provided at runtime — section deferred.

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of early May 2026, QXO trades at approximately $19.30 per share, implying a market capitalization of roughly $14 billion (based on approximately 725 million shares outstanding). [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: QXO Historical Prices / Date: 2026-05-04]** [Source: MarketWatch / Title: QXO Stock Price / Date: 2026-05-04]**

The current valuation appears to bake in the following assumptions:
1. **Deal Closure**: That both Kodiak and TopBuild close on schedule and without material regulatory/friction costs.
2. **Pro Forma Scale**: That the combined entity achieves the stated >$18 billion revenue and >$2 billion adjusted EBITDA run rate.
3. **Synergy Realization**: That management can extract hundreds of millions in cost and revenue synergies from TopBuild and Kodiak, justifying premium purchase multiples.
4. **Leverage Capacity**: That debt markets will absorb ~$6 billion in new acquisition financing and that the company can service this debt comfortably.
5. **Continued Capital Markets Access**: That the $3 billion Apollo/Temasek preferred commitment and equity markets remain open to fund the next leg of growth toward the $50 billion target.
6. **Margin Expansion**: That QXO can lift legacy Beacon’s EBITDA margin back toward or above historical levels while maintaining TopBuild’s higher-margin profile.

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
QXO is a capital-deployment roll-up in the North American building products distribution sector. The financial reality is not that of a steady-state distributor; it is an acquisition platform using equity and debt to consolidate a fragmented industry. The most meaningful financial lenses are pro forma leverage, EBITDA margin sustainability, integration synergy realization, and working capital efficiency—not traditional organic free cash flow yield.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)
- **Strong near-term liquidity and low leverage pre-TopBuild** ($2.4 billion cash, no ABL draw, net debt modest) — **High** confidence. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***
- **Founder-led M&A track record** (Brad Jacobs) with institutional backing (Apollo, Temasek) — **Medium** confidence.
- **Favorable debt maturity structure** (no maturities until 2032) — **High** confidence. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]***
- **TopBuild’s superior margin profile** (18%+ EBITDA margin) offers potential upside if preserved — **Medium** confidence.

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)
- **Negative GAAP earnings and heavy non-cash charges** (goodwill, intangibles, stock-based comp) — **High** confidence.
- **SG&A deleverage relative to legacy Beacon** (18.3% vs. 16.8%) suggesting integration costs are sticky — **Medium** confidence. [Source: QXO, Inc. 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-27]*** [Source: Beacon Roofing Supply 10-K / Title: FY2024 Annual Report / Date: 2025-02-27]***
- **Aggressive pace of M&A** creating operational indigestion risk — **Medium** confidence.
- **Substantial future dilution** from preferred conversions and stock-heavy deals — **High** confidence.

### Watch Items
- Q1 2026 earnings (expected mid-May 2026): first clean look at a full quarter of Beacon ownership and any Kodiak stub contribution.
- TopBuild shareholder vote and regulatory clearance; terms of the $6 billion financing package upon closing.
- Gross margin trend in legacy Beacon: whether Q4 2025’s 24.2% gross margin stabilizes or recovers toward Beacon’s historical 25.7%.
- Working capital: vendor rebates receivable and payable trends.
- Any 2026 quantitative guidance from management.

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

1. **Q1 2026 Financial Results**: Not yet reported as of May 5, 2026. Search scope: QXO investor relations, SEC EDGAR, Yahoo Finance earnings calendar. Analytical impact: inability to assess most recent quarterly momentum and Kodiak stub performance.
2. **Full FY2025 Detailed Income Statement**: While headline revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA are available, the complete 10-K income statement breakout (specifically operating income, interest expense, and tax benefit for the full year) was not fully extracted. Search scope: SEC EDGAR 10-K filing. Analytical impact: limits precise ROIC and interest-coverage calculations.
3. **Pro Forma Financial Statements for Kodiak and TopBuild**: No audited or unaudited pro forma balance sheet or income statement combining all three entities has been published as of the research date. Search scope: SEC EDGAR 8-K filings. Analytical impact: leverage and interest coverage are estimates rather than firm figures.
4. **ABL Facility Maturity and Covenants**: The exact maturity date and financial maintenance covenants of the ABL facility were not located. Search scope: QXO 10-K, credit agreements. Analytical impact: limits full assessment of refinancing risk.
5. **Segment-Level Profitability**: QXO reports as a single segment; no profitability data by residential vs. non-residential or by geography is disclosed. Search scope: 10-K segment footnote. Analytical impact: weakens ability to identify mix-driven margin drivers.
6. **Exact Common Shares Outstanding Post-January 2026 Offering**: Sources range from ~716 million to ~725 million. Search scope: QXO 10-Q or prospectus supplement. Analytical impact: market capitalization and dilution estimates carry a small margin of error.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: QXO Inc (QXO)

Research conducted: 2026-05-05

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

The dominant narrative around QXO over the past 90 days has been defined by two massive M&A milestones and the capital raises backing them, cementing the company’s transformation from a small technology shell into a North American building-products distribution giant.

**Closing of the $2.25 Billion Kodiak Building Partners Acquisition (April 1, 2026)**  
QXO completed its acquisition of Kodiak Building Partners, a lumber and building-materials distributor, from Court Square Capital Partners for approximately $2.25 billion ($2.0 billion in cash plus 13.2 million QXO shares, with a repurchase right at $40 per share). The deal expanded QXO’s addressable market to more than $200 billion and added roughly $2.4 billion in annual revenue. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "QXO to Buy Kodiak Building Partners for $2.25 Billion" / Date: 2026-02-11] ***  
[Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Completes Acquisition of Kodiak Building Partners" / Date: 2026-04-01] ***

**Announcement of the $17 Billion TopBuild Acquisition (April 19, 2026)**  
On April 19, 2026, QXO announced a definitive agreement to acquire TopBuild Corp. (NYSE: BLD), the largest distributor and installer of insulation and related building products in North America, for approximately $17 billion. The transaction—structured as roughly 45% cash and 55% QXO stock (subject to proration and a $505/share equivalent value)—is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, pending shareholder approvals from both companies and customary closing conditions. Management stated the deal would be “immediately and substantially accretive” and would create a combined entity with more than $18 billion in revenue and over $2 billion in adjusted EBITDA. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion" / Date: 2026-04-19] ***  
[Source: Reuters / Title: "QXO strikes $17 billion deal to acquire building products distributor TopBuild" / Date: 2026-04-20] **

**Upsized $3 Billion Convertible Preferred Equity Commitment (January 12, 2026)**  
QXO upsized its equity financing commitment led by Apollo Global Management and Temasek to $3 billion (via Series C convertible perpetual preferred stock) to fund future acquisitions. The commitment is available through July 15, 2026, extendable if a definitive acquisition agreement is signed before expiry. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion" / Date: 2026-01-12] ***  
[Source: Reuters / Title: "QXO adds $1.8 billion in firepower amid building products deals" / Date: 2026-01-12] **

**2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders (May 5, 2026)**  
The company is holding its annual meeting today (May 5, 2026), with shareholders voting on the election of seven directors, ratification of Deloitte & Touche as auditor, and an advisory say-on-pay vote. [Source: QXO SEC filing / Title: "Notice of 2026 Annual Meeting" / Date: 2026-03-24] ***

## 2. Earnings Call Deep Dive

*Note: Primary sourcing for Q4 2025 commentary is limited to company press releases and prepared remarks. A detailed transcript of the Q&A session was not located; the Q&A and pushback analysis below relies on inferred analyst concerns from estimate revisions and secondary reporting.*

### Headline Takeaways
QXO reported fourth-quarter 2025 net sales of $2.19 billion and a GAAP basic/diluted loss per share of $(0.17), driven by acquisition-related amortization and transaction costs. Adjusted diluted EPS came in at $0.02, matching the preliminary pre-announcement from January. Full-year 2025 revenue was $6.84 billion, including legacy Beacon results from the April 29, 2025 closing date. Adjusted EBITDA for the full year reached $647.8 million (9.5% margin), while adjusted gross margin was 24.9%. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results" / Date: 2026-02-25] ***

### Management Tone & Conviction
Brad Jacobs struck an unapologetically aggressive and confident tone, emphasizing execution velocity over near-term GAAP profitability. “Our fourth quarter results were in line with the pre-announcement we made last month,” Jacobs stated. “Operationally, we are executing against our integration plan across the legacy Beacon business, supported by disciplined investments in technology, sales capacity, and other high-return, long-term initiatives.” [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results" / Date: 2026-02-25] ***

On M&A, Jacobs framed the company as being in the early innings of a decade-long consolidation: “With Kodiak, we have grown our EBITDA run rate to more than $1 billion in under 10 months. Our acquisition pipeline remains very active, keeping us firmly on track to achieve $50 billion in annual revenue.” [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Reports Fourth Quarter 2025 Results" / Date: 2026-02-25] ***

### Q&A Pushback & Analyst Skepticism
While direct Q&A quotes were not retrievable, analyst skepticism appears to be surfacing through estimate behavior rather than rating downgrades. Consensus Q1 2026 revenue estimates were revised downward by approximately 12% over the trailing three months, and the Q1 2026 EPS consensus sits at $(0.09), a sharp reversal from the small adjusted profit in Q4. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *  
This suggests analysts are pressing on integration drag, seasonality in legacy Beacon, and the operating margin impact of absorbing Kodiak. Management’s insistence on “disciplined investments in technology” may be read by some as code for margin compression in the near term.

### Forward Commentary & Guidance
QXO did not issue explicit quantitative quarterly guidance in the Q4 press release. Instead, management reiterated the strategic goal of $50 billion in annual revenue over the coming decade and highlighted the Kodiak deal as “highly accretive” to 2026 earnings. The TopBuild announcement, which came after quarter-end, added another layer of forward accretion rhetoric, with $300 million in synergy targets by 2030. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion" / Date: 2026-04-19] ***

## 3. Analyst Coverage & Rating Changes

### Consensus Picture
As of early May 2026, QXO carries a unanimously bullish consensus: 14 covering analysts with a “Buy/Overweight” average rating and an average price target of approximately $32.69, implying roughly 50% upside to the recent $21.80 price level. The range of targets is not fully specified in available data, but recent actions cluster in the $30–$36 range. [Source: MarketWatch / Title: "QXO Inc. Analyst Estimates" / Date: 2026-05-01] *  
[Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *

### Specific Rating Actions in the Window
The majority of coverage was initiated during mid-2025 as the Beacon deal closed. More recent activity in the 90-day window includes:

- **Oppenheimer (April 27, 2026):** Maintained Outperform; raised price target to $32 from $25, citing the TopBuild deal as accretive. [Source: Futunn / Title: "Oppenheimer Maintains QXO Inc With Buy Rating, Raises Target Price to $32" / Date: 2026-04-27] *  
- **KeyBanc (April 20, 2026):** Maintained Overweight. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *  
- **RBC Capital (April 6, 2026):** Maintained Outperform. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *  
- **Barclays & Loop Capital (December 12, 2025):** Initiated Buy ratings. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *

Despite the bullish ratings, there is a subtle divergence: Q1 2026 revenue estimates have been revised down 12.12% over the past three months, and EPS estimates have dropped sharply. This creates a tension where the sell-side remains structurally positive but is trimming near-term expectations. [Source: ChartMill / Title: "QXO INC (QXO) Forecast, Price Target & Analyst Ratings" / Date: 2026-05-01] *

## 4. Regulatory, Legal & Compliance Developments

No active regulatory enforcement actions or material litigation driving the current narrative were identified. The Beacon acquisition received antitrust clearance in the U.S. and Canada in early 2025, removing a key regulatory overhang ahead of the April 2025 close. [Source: Yahoo Finance / Title: QXO news items regarding Beacon antitrust clearance / Date: 2025] *  
The TopBuild transaction has not yet raised publicly disclosed regulatory objections, though it will require HSR review and shareholder votes from both companies. No material legal proceedings were noted in the company’s 2025 10-K beyond customary acquisition-related disclosures. [Source: QXO Form 10-K / Title: "FORM 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2025" / Date: 2026-02-27] ***

## 5. Corporate Events: M&A, Leadership, Restructuring, Capital Actions

### M&A Activity
QXO’s corporate identity *is* M&A. Since Jacobs assumed control in mid-2024, the company has executed:
- **Beacon Roofing Supply:** $11 billion, closed April 29, 2025. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Completes Acquisition of Beacon Roofing Supply" / Date: 2025-04-29] ***  
- **Kodiak Building Partners:** $2.25 billion, closed April 1, 2026. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Completes Acquisition of Kodiak Building Partners" / Date: 2026-04-01] ***  
- **TopBuild Corp.:** $17 billion, announced April 19, 2026; expected close Q3 2026. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion" / Date: 2026-04-19] ***

### Leadership Changes
On December 15, 2025, Brad Jacobs announced he would step down as chairman of XPO and GXO Logistics effective December 31, 2025, to focus exclusively on QXO and Jacobs Private Equity. Jacobs stated: “By transitioning out of my board positions at XPO and GXO, I can dedicate even more energy to QXO... We intend to grow QXO into a $50 billion revenue leader.” [Source: QXO press release / Title: "Brad Jacobs to Step Down from Chairman Roles at XPO and GXO Logistics" / Date: 2025-12-15] ***

In November 2024, QXO hired Ashwin Rao (formerly Target’s head of AI) as Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer, signaling the tech-enabled dimension of the rollup strategy. [Source: HBS Dealer / Title: "QXO hires a chief artificial intelligence officer" / Date: 2024-11-07] *

### Restructuring & Workforce Actions
No discrete workforce reduction announcements were identified. However, integration of Beacon, Kodiak, and eventually TopBuild implies significant back-office rationalization over time. Management has cited “network optimization” and “AI-powered inventory management” as synergy levers rather than headline restructuring events. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Buy Kodiak Building Partners for $2.25 Billion" / Date: 2026-02-11] ***

### Capital Return Announcements
No dividends or share buyback authorizations were announced. The company is in pure growth-investment mode. The Kodiak deal includes a share repurchase right at $40 per share on the 13.2 million shares issued, but this is deal-specific and not a general capital-return program. [Source: Business Wire / Title: "QXO to Buy Kodiak Building Partners for $2.25 Billion" / Date: 2026-02-11] ***

### Material Insider Transactions
No unusual or material open-market insider transactions for QXO itself were located in the research window. SEC Form 4 filings retrieved were predominantly legacy Beacon awards vesting pre-merger. Routine 10b5-1 activity, if any, was not flagged as anomalous.

## 6. Sentiment & Positioning

### Institutional Sentiment
The most significant institutional signal is the $3 billion convertible preferred commitment from Apollo, Temasek, and affiliated investors, announced January 12, 2026. This is a high-conviction capital infusion designed to fund qualifying acquisitions through July 15, 2026. It functions as both a vote of confidence in the rollup thesis and a potential future dilution overhang. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion" / Date: 2026-01-12] ***  
No detailed 13F position changes were available in the window, though the Apollo/Temasek commitment suggests smart-money sponsorship of the M&A pipeline.

### Retail & Social Sentiment
Retail sentiment is present but lower-signal. The r/qxo subreddit includes discussion of a potential short squeeze, with users calculating implied peg prices based on the TopBuild exchange ratio ($505 / 20.2 shares = ~$25.00). [Source: Reddit / Title: r/qxo discussion threads / Date: 2026] (No tag)  
This retail chatter has not demonstrably moved the stock independent of fundamental catalysts and should be treated as noise rather than a primary positioning driver.

### Short Interest
Short interest data is conflicted across providers as of late April 2026:
- **Finviz / Seeking Alpha (Apr 15, 2026):** ~74.88 million shares short, representing roughly 10.4%–11.4% of shares outstanding and approximately 8.5–9.3 days to cover. [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: QXO Stock Price, Quote and News / Date: 2026-05-01] *  
[Source: Finviz / Title: QXO Short Interest / Date: 2026-04-15] *  
- **StockAnalysis (Apr 20, 2026):** Only 5.85 million shares short (0.81% of shares outstanding), which appears inconsistent with other aggregators and may reflect a data lag or float-calculation difference. [Source: StockAnalysis / Title: "QXO, Inc. (QXO) Statistics & Valuation" / Date: 2026-04-20] *  
- **Third-party blog (January 2026):** Listed QXO short interest at 22% of float in a “most shorted stocks” ranking; given the date and lack of corroboration, this is treated as stale or methodology-specific. [Source: Gainify.io / Title: "Short Squeeze Stocks to Watch in 2026" / Date: 2026-01-06] (No tag)

The most credible near-term figure is the 10–11% range, which is elevated and indicates a material institutional bear contingent betting against execution risk, balance-sheet strain, or dilution.

### Valuation Narrative
The market is wrestling with two conflicting frames:
1. **The “Jacobs Premium”:** Bulls argue QXO deserves a growth multiple because it is replicating the United Rentals / XPO playbook in a fragmented $800 billion industry, using AI and scale to expand margins from the mid-single digits toward double digits.
2. **The “Rollup Risk” Discount:** Bears point to GAAP losses ($279.4 million net loss in 2025), negative GAAP EPS, rising debt ($3.91 billion per recent data), and the sheer speed of deal-making as reasons for caution. The stock trades at a forward P/E near 55x on NTM estimates and a negative trailing P/E, leaving little room for execution error. [Source: CNBC / Title: QXO Inc - Stock Price, Quote and News / Date: 2026-03-06] *  
[Source: StockAnalysis / Title: "QXO, Inc. (QXO) Statistics & Valuation" / Date: 2026-04-20] *

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

No dedicated short-seller research reports or activist 13D filings were identified in the research window. The elevated short interest appears to be driven by generalized skepticism around integration risk and balance-sheet leverage rather than a public short-thesis campaign. No activist investor involvement was located. [Search scope: web search for "QXO short seller report," "QXO activist investor," "QXO 13D activist" with no relevant primary or secondary hits.]

## 8. Narrative Shifts

A clear, identifiable narrative shift has occurred over the past 12 months:

- **Prior Narrative (2024–early 2025):** “Can Brad Jacobs actually build a real company, or is this a promotional SPAC-style shell game?” The story was speculative, centered on a tiny software company with grandiose plans and no revenue base.
- **Current Narrative (mid-2025–present):** “Jacobs has built a real business with real scale, but can he integrate it all before leverage or operational chaos breaks it?” The narrative shifted from *skepticism about existence* to *skepticism about sustainability* after the Beacon close, the $6.8 billion revenue print, and the rapid-fire Kodiak/TopBuild announcements.

**Trigger:** The April 2025 closing of the Beacon acquisition and the subsequent Q4 2025 revenue and EBITDA figures proved the company could transact at scale. The January 2026 $3 billion Apollo/Temasek financing and the April 2026 TopBuild deal cemented the new narrative: QXO is now a serious, if highly leveraged, industry consolidator.

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

A material disconnect exists:

**Consensus Optimism vs. GAAP Profitability and Estimate Cuts:** Sell-side analysts are uniformly bullish (100% Buy/Overweight) with price targets implying ~50% upside. Yet the company is still reporting GAAP net losses, Q1 2026 consensus EPS is negative, and revenue estimates for the immediate quarter have been revised meaningfully lower. The stock also carries elevated short interest (~10–11%), signaling that a significant market cohort disagrees with the sell-side euphoria. The disconnect suggests the bull narrative is pricing in flawless execution of multiple simultaneous integrations and synergies that have not yet materialized in reported earnings.

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization
QXO is currently perceived as the most aggressive rollup story in U.S. industrials—a Brad Jacobs-led, private-equity-style consolidation of building-products distribution wrapped in a public-company multiple. The dominant market story is one of breathtaking scale attainment ($6.8B revenue, $1B+ EBITDA run rate in under a year) colliding with equally breathtaking execution risk.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **M&A Velocity as Strategy (High Confidence):** The company has closed $13B+ in deals in 11 months and announced another $17B. This is well-evidenced and is the core of the investment thesis. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion" / Date: 2026-04-19] ***
2. **Apollo/Temasek Financing Validation (High Confidence):** The $3 billion preferred commitment is a concrete institutional endorsement of the deal pipeline. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion" / Date: 2026-01-12] ***
3. **Integration and Synergy Execution (Medium Confidence):** Management repeatedly cites margin expansion via AI, procurement scale, and network optimization. Evidence is limited to prepared remarks; Beacon integration is ongoing but not independently verified.
4. **Tech-Enabled Differentiation (Medium Confidence):** The hiring of Ashwin Rao and repeated references to “AI-powered inventory management” create a narrative of tech-driven margin expansion. Whether this translates to measurable outperformance versus traditional distributors remains unproven. [Source: HBS Dealer / Title: "QXO hires a chief artificial intelligence officer" / Date: 2024-11-07] *
5. **Accretion from TopBuild and Kodiak (Low-Medium Confidence):** Management frames both deals as “highly accretive,” but the TopBuild deal is not yet closed and carries significant share issuance and leverage risk.

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **Integration Indigestion / Margin Erosion (High Confidence):** Acquiring $13B+ in revenue in under a year while simultaneously pursuing a $17B deal raises the risk of operational strain, customer attrition, and missed synergy targets.
2. **Balance Sheet Stress / Dilution (High Confidence):** The company has $3.91 billion in debt and a negative net cash position. The Apollo/Temasek preferred stock is convertible, creating a future dilution overhang. The TopBuild deal is 55% stock, further diluting common equity.
3. **Analyst Estimate Downgrades Breaking Bull Momentum (Medium Confidence):** Q1 2026 revenue estimates have already been cut ~12%. If Q1 earnings on May 8–12 show further deterioration or weak guidance, the unanimous bullish rating wall could crack.
4. **Regulatory/Shareholder Rejection of TopBuild (Low-Medium Confidence):** While both boards approved the deal, shareholder votes and HSR review remain. Given the size, antitrust scrutiny is possible, though not yet evident.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **May 5, 2026:** Annual Meeting of Stockholders (today). [Source: QXO SEC filing / Title: "Notice of 2026 Annual Meeting" / Date: 2026-03-24] ***
- **May 8–12, 2026:** Q1 2026 earnings release and call (dates conflict between May 8 and May 12 across data providers). [Source: CNBC / Title: QXO quote page / Date: 2026-03-06] *; [Source: ChartMill / Title: QXO earnings calendar / Date: 2026-05-01] *
- **Q3 2026 (estimated):** Expected closing of the TopBuild acquisition, subject to shareholder approvals and regulatory clearance. [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO to Acquire TopBuild for $17 Billion" / Date: 2026-04-19] ***
- **July 15, 2026:** Expiration of the initial Apollo/Temasek Series C preferred commitment period (extendable if a definitive acquisition agreement is in place). [Source: QXO press release / Title: "QXO Upsizes Convertible Preferred Equity Placement to $3 Billion" / Date: 2026-01-12] ***

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Full Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript:** No primary transcript of the Q&A session was located. Searches of earnings call databases, company IR pages, and financial news aggregators returned only prepared remarks. The characterization of analyst pushback and management tone during Q&A is therefore inferred from estimate revisions and press-release language rather than direct quotes. *Analytical impact:* Limits ability to assess management defensiveness or hedging on guidance.
- **Verified Short Interest / Borrow Cost Data:** Short interest figures conflict sharply across aggregators (5.85M vs. 74.88M shares short). The Finviz/Seeking Alpha figure (~74.88M / ~11%) is treated as the more credible timestamped data, but a definitive primary source (e.g., exchange-reported bi-monthly data) was not retrieved. *Analytical impact:* Confidence in short-interest characterization is reduced.
- **QXO-Specific Insider Transaction Data:** Searches for Form 4 filings for QXO insiders post-Beacon close returned no material open-market purchases or sales. Available SEC Form 4 data was dominated by legacy Beacon equity award exercises pre-merger. *Analytical impact:* Cannot assess whether management is buying into the narrative with personal capital.
- **13F Positioning Detail:** No specific 13F filings showing discrete hedge-fund initiations or exits in Q1 2026 were available. *Analytical impact:* Institutional sentiment analysis relies heavily on the Apollo/Temasek preferred commitment as a proxy.
- **Earnings Date Certainty for Q1 2026:** Sources conflict between May 8, May 12, and May 15, 2026. *Analytical impact:* Catalyst dating is approximate pending official confirmation.
